name: Sampada Ingalgi
title: Senior Clinical Data Programmer | CDISC SDTM Developer | EDC Migration Specialist
summary: Senior Clinical Data Programmer with 4+ years of experience in EDC platform migrations, clinical database development, and CDISC SDTM implementation for global pharmaceutical clients. Proven expertise in Oracle InForm, REDCap Cloud, and Oracle Rave, with hands-on experience in end-to-end EDC migrations, study build, CRF design review, metadata assessment, and data validation. Strong background in SDTM IG dataset development, regulatory-ready deliverables, and UAT/post-migration support. Recognized for technical mentoring, multi-study management, and cross-functional stakeholder collaboration while consistently meeting aggressive timelines.

highlights: Led end-to-end Oracle InForm to REDCap Cloud migrations, owning study design review, CRF comparison, metadata assessment, and full REDCap Cloud study build, delivering regulatory-compliant outputs on schedule.
Championed UAT support and post-migration data review, resolving issues and validating completeness prior to study go-live
Enforced CDISC standards, SOPs, and ICH-GCP compliance as migration quality owner across all study deliverables
Mentored junior programmers on EDC migration workflows and validation best practices, elevating team capability and delivery quality
Partnered with global cross-functional stakeholders to drive migration milestones and resolve delivery blockers on schedule
Executed data mapping, migration validation, and testing across multiple studies, ensuring high data integrity aligned with CDISC and ICH-GCP requirements
Led multiple Oracle InForm migrations (4.x to 7.x) via DMW, consistently meeting tight regulatory timelines across concurrent studies.
Built a custom data comparison tool to validate source-to-migrated data integrity during Oracle InForm (4.x to 7.x) upgrades using DMW (Data Management Workbench)
Architected SDTM-compliant datasets (IG 3.1.1) from raw CRF data, producing submission-ready outputs aligned with CDISC specifications
Owned the full SDTM lifecycle — dataset generation, independent QC, and validation — maintaining zero-defect quality standards across the study portfolio
Developed robust SDTM and analytical programming solutions in direct support of regulatory submissions
Reviewed E2E, milestone, and protocol-driven reports with technical precision, delivering quality-focused feedback aligned with CDISC standards
Maintained audit-ready deliverables through rigorous adherence to SOPs, internal data standards, and project governance requirements
Managed a high-volume concurrent study portfolio without compromising data quality or delivery timelines.
Mentored junior SDTM programmers on validation workflows and CDISC best practices, accelerating team proficiency
Created dashboard to track the progress of migration studies using PowerBI

education.institution: Brindavan College of Engineering, VTU, Bangalore
VVS Sardar Patel High School, Bangalore
